raw_text:
Mid Data Scientist

Catalyst Labs — Turning data into intelligence, insights into impact.

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly, 3 days/week on-site)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$125,000 - $155,000 USD per year

The Opportunity

Catalyst Labs is seeking a Mid Data Scientist to join our Analytics team. In this role, you'll design and

execute A/B tests, build statistical models for product recommendations, and develop data pipelines

that drive business decisions for our clients. You'll work cross-functionally with product and

engineering teams to translate business questions into rigorous data science solutions. This is an

ideal opportunity for someone with 2-5 years of experience looking to grow their skills in

experimentation, causal inference, and machine learning at scale.

About Catalyst Labs

Catalyst Labs is a fast-growing data intelligence platform founded in 2019 that helps mid-market SaaS
companies unlock actionable insights from their user behavior data. We build ML-powered analytics
tools that enable product teams to make faster, data-driven decisions. Our platform processes millions

of events daily and serves over 200 enterprise clients.

Day-to-Day

Design and analyze A/B tests and multivariate experiments for product features; calculate power
analysis, define metrics, and present statistical findings to stakeholders

Build predictive models using scikit-learn and statistical methods (e.g., logistic regression,
ensemble techniques) to forecast user churn and guide retention strategies

Write optimized SQL queries and Python (Pandas/NumPy) scripts to extract, transform, and

analyze large datasets from our data warehouse

Develop and maintain reproducible data pipelines using Python and SQL to ensure data quality and
automated reporting for key business metrics

Collaborate with product managers and engineers to scope data science projects, define success

metrics, and iterate on model performance

Communicate findings and recommendations to non-technical stakeholders through visualizations,
dashboards, and written reports

Mentor junior analysts on statistical best practices, experimental design, and Python data analysis
techniques

Contribute to the evaluation and adoption of new tools and frameworks to improve model
deployment and monitoring

You Have

 2-5 years of professional experience in data science, analytics, or a quantitative role

Strong proficiency in Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) for data manipulation and statistical

modeling

Solid SQL skills with ability to write complex queries for data exploration and feature engineering

Working knowledge of statistical concepts: hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals,

and p-values

Experience with experimentation and causal inference (e.g., randomized controlled trials,

difference-in-differences)

Ability to communicate technical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences

Bonus

Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Plotly, or similar) and dashboarding

Familiarity with machine learning model deployment, MLOps, or model monitoring in production

environments

Prior experience in SaaS, product analytics, or growth-focused roles

Knowledge of Bayesian statistics or advanced experimentation techniques

Our Stack

Python   Pandas   NumPy   scikit-learn   SQL   Jupyter Notebooks   Git/GitHub   Tableau

Apache Spark (PySpark)   Statsmodels (for statistical modeling)

What We Offer

Competitive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% employer coverage

Flexible PTO policy with a minimum of 20 days per year

Professional development budget of $2,500/year for courses, conferences, or certifications

Collaborative remote-friendly work environment with modern tools and collaborative spaces

Equity options (stock grant with 4-year vesting schedule)

Free lunch and beverages in the San Francisco office; meal stipend for remote days

Wellness program including gym membership reimbursement and mental health support

Parental leave and family support benefits

Apply

To apply, submit your resume, a cover letter describing your experience with A/B testing or statistical
modeling, and a link to your GitHub or a portfolio of past work to: careers@catalystlabs.io. Please
include 'Mid Data Scientist - [Your Name]' in the subject line. We review applications on a rolling basis.
nice_to_have: Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Plotly, or similar) and dashboarding
Familiarity with machine learning model deployment, MLOps, or model monitoring in production
Prior experience in SaaS, product analytics, or growth-focused roles
Knowledge of Bayesian statistics or advanced experimentation techniques
